sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the full picture. Let me analyze the doors:
1. Top-left panel: c door at [[8, 8], [8, 12], [15, 12], [15, 8]] - 8x5 frame (rows 8-15, cols 8-12), interior 6x3
2. Top-right panel: c door at [[38, 53], [38, 60], [42, 60], [42, 53]] - 5x8 frame (rows 38-42, cols 53-60), interior 3x6
3. Bottom-left panel: c door at [[41, 5], [41, 12], [48, 12], [48, 5]] - 8x8 frame (rows 41-48, cols 5-12), interior 6x6
4. Bottom-right panel: c door at [[50, 50], [50, 54], [54, 54], [54, 50]] - 5x5 frame (rows 50-54, cols 50-54), interior 3x3
And the agents:
1. N+c horizontal: [[39, 54], [39, 59], [41, 59], [41, 54]] - 3x6 (rows 39-41, cols 54-59) - in door 2 (top-right)
2. N+c vertical: [[42, 30], [42, 32], [47, 32], [47, 30]] - 6x3 (rows 42-47, cols 30-32) - in bottom-right panel, needs door 1 (top-left)
3. N+B square: [[45, 42], [45, 47], [50, 47], [50, 42]] - 6x6 (rows 45-50, cols 42-47) - in bottom-right panel, needs door 3 (bottom-left)
4. N+W small: [[51, 51], [51, 53], [53, 53], [53, 51]] - 3x3 (rows 51-53, cols 51-53) - in door 4 (bottom-right)
So the matching is:
- N+c horizontal (3x6) → door 2 (3x6 interior) ✓ already placed
- N+c vertical (6x3) → door 1 (6x3 interior) - needs to go to top-left panel
- N+B square (6x6) → door 3 (6x6 interior) - needs to go to bottom-left panel
- N+W small (3x3) → door 4 (3x3 interior) ✓ already placed
The N+c vertical and N+B square agents are in the bottom-right panel, but their matching doors are in other panels. They can't cross the purple barrier directly.
But wait - the N+c vertical agent is at cols 30-32, which is at the boundary between the bottom-left and bottom-right panels. The purple barrier is at col 30. So the N+c vertical agent is partially in the bottom-left panel (cols 30-32, but the panel boundary is at col 30).
Actually, looking at the segmentation, the bottom-left panel is [[30, 0], [30, 20], [62, 20], [62, 0]] - rows 30-62, cols 0-20. The bottom-right panel is [[30, 30], [30, 62], [62, 62], [62, 30]] - rows 30-62, cols 30-62. So the panels are adjacent at col 20-30, with a purple barrier in between.
The N+c vertical agent is at cols 30-32, which is in the bottom-right panel. To reach door 1 in the top-left panel (cols 8-12), it would need to cross the purple barrier.
Hmm, but the N+c vertical agent is at the edge of the bottom-right panel (cols 30-32). Maybe I can move it LEFT to cross into the bottom-left panel, and then UP to reach door 1 in the top-left panel.
Wait, but the purple barrier is at cols 20-30 (between the bottom-left and bottom-right panels). The N+c vertical agent is at cols 30-32, so it's just inside the bottom-right panel. If I move it LEFT, it would hit the purple barrier.
Actually, looking at the image, there's a gap in the purple barrier at the junction between the four panels. The N+c vertical agent is positioned near this gap. Maybe I can move it through the gap.
Let me try moving the N+c vertical agent LEFT to see if it can cross into the bottom-left panel.
But first, I need to make the N+c vertical agent the active agent. Currently, the N+W agent is active (it has the white center). I need to click on the N+c vertical agent to swap its center with the N+W agent, making it the active agent.
Wait, but the N+W agent is inside door 2. If I click on the N+c vertical agent, the swap would move the N+W agent out of door 2. That might not be what I want.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the goal is to place ALL agents in their matching doors, not just the N+W agent. The N+W agent is already in door 2, but the other agents need to be moved to their doors.
To move the N+c vertical agent to door 1 (top-left panel), I need to:
1. Make it the active agent (click on it)
2. Move it LEFT through the gap, then UP to door 1
But clicking on it would swap its center with the N+W agent, making the N+W agent have a c center and the N+c vertical agent have a W center. That would mess up the matching.
Hmm, maybe the click mechanic doesn't swap centers, but something else. Let me re-examine what happened in level 1.
Actually, in level 1, I clicked on the N+W agent to make it active, then moved it to the door. The click swapped the centers, but then the level completed. So maybe the center swap is part of the puzzle.
Wait, in level 1, there was only one N agent (N+W). Clicking on it didn't swap anything because there was no other N agent to swap with. In level 2, there are four N agents, and clicking on one swaps its center with the active agent.
So the click mechanic swaps centers between N agents. The goal might be to have each agent's center color match some criterion, not necessarily the door's frame color.
Let me reconsider. In level 1, the N+W agent was moved to the door, and the level completed. The agent's center was W, and the door's frame was c. So the center color doesn't need to match the door's frame color.
In level 2, the N+W agent is in door 2 (3x3), and the N+c horizontal agent is in door 1 (3x6). Both are in their shape-matching doors. But the level hasn't completed. Maybe the goal is to have ALL agents in their shape-matching doors.
The N+c vertical (6x3) needs to go to door 1 in the top-left panel (6x3 interior).
The N+B square (6x6) needs to go to door 3 in the bottom-left panel (6x6 interior).
To move these agents, I need to make them active by clicking on them. But clicking swaps centers. So I need to plan the center swaps carefully.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the click mechanic. When I click on an N agent, it swaps its center with the active agent. But what if I click on the same agent twice? The first click swaps centers, and the second click swaps them back. So I can temporarily make an agent active, move it, and then swap back.
But that's complicated. Let me first try to move the N+c vertical agent to its door and see what happens.
The N+c vertical agent is at rows 42-47, cols 30-32. Door 1 is at rows 8-15, cols 8-12 (interior rows 9-14, cols 9-11). To get there, the agent needs to move LEFT and UP.
But the agent is in the bottom-right panel, and door 1 is in the top-left panel. The agent needs to cross the purple barrier. Looking at the image, there's a gap at the junction of the four panels (around rows 30, cols 20-30). The N+c vertical agent is at cols 30-32, which is near this gap.
Let me try moving the N+c vertical agent LEFT to see if it can cross into the bottom-left panel.
First, I need to make the N+c vertical agent active. I'll click on it.
